title: Copilot turns package evidence into a Clean Core backlog
subtitle: Copilot replay for package-level Clean Core planning: enumerate objects, run ATC, read release state, and group backlog items.
outcome: A grouped modernization backlog with risk levels and SAP evidence.

start.text: Analyze package ZSD_LEGACY for Clean Core and ABAP Cloud risks. Do not change code. Group findings into a modernization backlog.

package.summary: ARC-1 starts with package inventory, not a single hand-picked class.
package.result: 28 custom objects found: 9 CLAS, 3 INTF, 5 PROG, 6 DDLS, 2 TABL, 2 MSAG, 1 SRVD. 7 objects are in $TMP and need ownership cleanup.

atc.summary: ATC gives the package-wide cloud-readiness signal used by the Clean Core skill.

api-state.summary: ARC-1 reads release-state evidence so the backlog has successor direction.
api-state.result: USR02: not released. VBAK/VBAP: direct table access not cloud-ready. BUT000: use released business partner APIs or released interface views where applicable.

items.label: USR02
items.value: internal; replace with released user API/view

where-used.summary: Where-used evidence separates high-risk central utilities from isolated cleanup candidates.
where-used.result: ZCL_SD_ORDER_READER is used by three reports and one service. ZCL_SD_USER_LOOKUP is used by batch job ZSD_SYNC_USERS only.

options.label: Backlog board
options.description: Group findings into execution-ready modernization items.

panel.title: Modernization backlog
panel.body: The backlog is grouped by fixability rather than by raw ATC severity. That makes the output useful for planning instead of a long finding dump.

items.label: Priority 1
items.value: Replace direct table readers in central utilities

items.label: Priority 2
items.value: Refactor unreleased API calls with released successors

items.label: Priority 3
items.value: Move $TMP objects into owned packages or retire

exec.text: Teams summary: ZSD_LEGACY is not clean-core ready yet. Highest risk is central direct table access in order and user lookup utilities. I recommend a two-sprint plan: first replace central readers with released APIs/views, then clean up ownership and likely-unused reports. No code was changed.

done.text: This replay mirrors the package-level Clean Core skill: package inventory, ATC, API release state, where-used priority, then a human-owned backlog.
